Package index

Dataset I/O

Read and write clinical datasets without a Java or SAS dependency. read_xpt() and write_xpt() handle SAS transport files in pure R. read_json() and write_json() support CDISC Dataset-JSON v1.1. read_parquet() and write_parquet() use Arrow when available. convert_dataset() moves between formats while preserving labels, lengths, types, and dataset-level metadata.

read_xpt() stable
Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame
write_xpt() stable
Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file
read_json()
Read a CDISC Dataset-JSON file
write_json()
Write a data frame as CDISC Dataset-JSON v1.1
read_parquet()
Read an Apache Parquet dataset with CDISC column attributes
write_parquet()
Write a data frame to Apache Parquet with CDISC column attributes
convert_dataset()
Convert a dataset between XPT, Dataset-JSON, and Parquet

Specification Metadata

Build and inspect herald_spec objects: the metadata source for dataset labels, variable labels, storage lengths, expected types, standards, and dataset classes. apply_spec() stamps those attributes onto data frames before validation and transport writing.

as_herald_spec() experimental
Construct a herald_spec object
herald_spec() experimental
Construct a rich herald_spec with all submission slots
is_herald_spec()
Is x a herald_spec?
apply_spec() stable
Stamp column and dataset attributes from a herald_spec

Define-XML 2.1

Read, write, and render Define-XML 2.1. The rich herald_spec() constructor carries study metadata, datasets, variables, codelists, methods, comments, document leaves, value-level metadata, and ADaM ARM. Parsed Define-XML can be passed to validate() so metadata-dependent rules run with the same context as reviewers.

read_define_xml() stable
Read a Define-XML 2.1 file
write_define_xml()
Write a Define-XML 2.1 file from a herald specification
write_define_html()
Write a Define-XML 2.1 HTML rendering
validate_spec()
Validate a herald_spec for Define-XML completeness

Validation Engine

validate() runs the compiled rule catalog against directories or named lists of data frames and returns a herald_result. Rule filters can target exact IDs, authorities, standards, and study-specific severity policies. rule_catalog() and supported_standards() expose the compiled corpus for planning, QC, and coverage review.

validate() stable
Validate CDISC clinical data against the conformance rule catalog
rule_catalog() experimental
Compiled rule catalog
supported_standards()
Summarise herald's standards coverage

Reports

Convert herald_result objects into submission-review artifacts. report() dispatches from file extension; explicit writers produce self-contained HTML, workbook-style XLSX, and machine-readable JSON. Reports include findings, rule counts, dataset metadata, skipped references, and run metadata.

report() stable
Write a herald_result to disk in any supported format
write_report_html() experimental
Write a herald_result as a self-contained HTML report
write_report_xlsx() experimental
Write a herald_result as a five-sheet XLSX workbook
write_report_json() experimental
Write a herald_result as canonical JSON

Controlled Terminology

Load bundled SDTM and ADaM controlled terminology, inspect release metadata, list available NCI EVS releases, and refresh local caches. ct_provider() exposes CDISC CT through the same dictionary-provider protocol used by SRS, MedDRA, WHODrug, LOINC, SNOMED, and sponsor dictionaries.

load_ct()
Load bundled or cached CDISC Controlled Terminology
ct_info()
Summarise the currently resolvable CT.
available_ct_releases()
List available CDISC CT releases
download_ct()
Download + cache a CDISC CT release from NCI EVS
ct_provider() experimental
CDISC Controlled Terminology as a Dictionary Provider

External Dictionary Providers

Register external or sponsor-owned terminology sources for validation runs. Providers expose contains() and optional lookup() methods so rules can check dictionary membership without knowing the file format. Use session-wide registration for interactive work or pass providers directly to validate(dictionaries = ...) for reproducible pipelines.

new_dict_provider() experimental
Construct a dictionary-provider object
custom_provider() experimental
Generic in-memory Dictionary Provider
register_dictionary() experimental
Install a dictionary provider in the session registry
unregister_dictionary() experimental
Remove a dictionary from the session registry
list_dictionaries()
List known dictionaries
download_srs()
Download + cache the FDA SRS / UNII table
srs_provider() experimental
FDA SRS / UNII as a Dictionary Provider
meddra_provider() experimental
MedDRA as a Dictionary Provider
whodrug_provider() experimental
WHO-Drug as a Dictionary Provider
loinc_provider() experimental
LOINC as a Dictionary Provider
snomed_provider() experimental
SNOMED CT as a Dictionary Provider

Submission Discovery and ADaM Helpers

Helpers for classifying ADaM datasets and detecting analysis dataset shapes from variable names. These functions are useful for routing class-scoped ADaM rules and for checking whether a sponsor dataset looks like ADSL, BDS, OCCDS, or ADTTE.

detect_adam_class()
Detect the ADaM dataset class from column names
detect_adam_classes()
Detect ADaM class for each dataset

Object Methods

Print and summary methods for the main herald objects. These keep console output compact while preserving the full object for programmatic access in pipelines and reports.

print(<herald_define>)
Print a herald_define
print(<herald_dict_provider>)
Print a herald_dict_provider
print(<herald_result>)
Print a herald_result
print(<herald_spec>)
Print a herald_spec
summary(<herald_result>)
Summarise a herald_result

Operators

The validation engine dispatches each rule to a registered operator – a vectorised predicate with signature op(data, ctx, ...). Operators are referenced by name from rule YAML and resolved at run time. The full sortable catalog of built-in operators, their kinds, arg schemas, and cost hints lives in the operator catalog vignette.

herald-ops-catalog
Operator catalog

Built-in Datasets

Lazy-loaded SDTM and ADaM datasets and specs shipped with the package. Use directly in examples and vignettes without readRDS() or system.file(). The pilot-data page documents the legacy .rds fixtures retained under inst/extdata for backwards compatibility.

dm
Pilot DM (Demographics) domain
adsl
Pilot ADSL (Subject-Level Analysis Dataset)
adae
Pilot ADAE (Adverse Events Analysis Dataset, OCCDS)
advs
Pilot ADVS (Vital Signs Analysis Dataset, BDS)
sdtm_spec
Pilot SDTM specification (herald_spec)
adam_spec
Pilot ADaM specification (herald_spec)
pilot-data
CDISC Pilot 03 example data and specs (bundled)

On this page
